name: Dummy Deck
description: Sandbox for testing new technical phrases before migration.

cards.sideA: Estate atento por si llega el paquete.

sideB.text: Keep an eye out for the package.
Keep an eye out for the delivery.
Please keep an eye out for the package.
Keep an eye out for the package arriving.
Keep an eye out for the package to arrive.

cards.note:
**[misc:1]**

~Keep an eye out for the package arrives~ is not valid because 'for' is a preposition and cannot be followed directly by a subject and conjugated verb. You should use an '-ing' form or an infinitive, like [Keep an eye out for the package arriving] or [Keep an eye out for the package to arrive].

~Keep an eye on for the package~ is incorrect because it mixes two different expressions. You must either use [Keep an eye on the package] (if you are watching the physical package itself) or [Keep an eye out for the package] (if you are waiting for it to arrive).

~Keep an eye on the package to arrive~ is incorrect because you cannot use 'keep an eye on' followed by an infinitive clause for an expected event. You must use [keep an eye out for] when waiting for something to arrive.

cards.sideA: Por favor, mantenme informado sobre cualquier novedad.

sideB.text: Please keep me in the loop on any updates.
Please keep me in the loop about any updates.
Please keep me in the loop on anything new.
Please keep me in the loop on any news.
Please keep me in the loop about any news.
Please let me know about any news.

cards.note:
**[misc:10]**

~Please keep me on the loop about any updates~ is incorrect because the standard idiom uses the preposition [in], as in [keep me in the loop].

~Please keep me inform about any news~ is incorrect because the verb 'keep' requires the past participle [informed] when used as an adjective for a state, not the base verb.

cards.sideA: La política restringe a los empleados el acceso a esos datos.

sideB.text: The policy restricts employees from accessing that data.
The policy prohibits employees from accessing that data.
The policy prevents employees from accessing that data.
The policy restricts employees from having access to that data.

cards.note:
**[er:225]**

~The policy doesn't allow employees from accessing that data~ is incorrect because the verb 'allow' requires an infinitive [to access], not 'from' plus a gerund. Only verbs of restriction like restrict, prevent, or stop take [from + -ing].

cards.sideA: Un buen modelo sirve como plano para el desarrollo del software.

sideB.text: A good model serves as a blueprint for software development.
A good model acts as a blueprint for software development.
A good model works as a blueprint for developing software.
A good model is like a blueprint for software development.

cards.sideA: Esta clase base comparte su herencia con los controladores hijos.

sideB.text: This base class shares its inheritance with the child controllers.
This base class shares its inheritance with child controllers.
The base class shares its inheritance with the child controllers.

cards.note:
~This base class shares its inheritance with the children controllers~ is not valid because when a noun acts as an adjective to modify another noun, it typically remains in its singular form. Use [child controllers].

~This base class shares its inherent with the children controllers~ is incorrect because 'inherent' is an adjective, but the sentence requires the noun [inheritance]. Also, use [child] instead of 'children'.

cards.sideA: Los niños prometieron portarse bien.

sideB.text: The children promised to behave.
The kids promised to behave themselves.
The children made a promise to behave.
The children promised to behave well.

cards.note: ~The children promised to get along~ changes the meaning. 'Get along' means to have a good relationship with someone (llevarse bien). To specifically say 'portarse bien', you should use [behave].

cards.sideA: Ella aceptó el premio en nombre del equipo.

sideB.text: She accepted the award on behalf of the team.
She accepted the prize on behalf of the team.
She received the award on behalf of the team.

cards.note:
~She accepted the award on the team behalf~ is not valid because it requires a possessive form, such as [on the team's behalf] or [on behalf of the team].

~She accepted the award on the team~ and ~in the team~ are incorrect because they imply physical location rather than representation.

cards.sideA: Por favor, hablen en representación de quienes no pueden.

sideB.text: Please speak on behalf of those who cannot.
Please speak up for those who can't.
Please speak on behalf of those who can't.
Please speak on behalf of people who cannot.

cards.note:
~Please speak on behalf who cannot~ is missing the preposition 'of'. The correct phrase is [on behalf of].

~Please speak on behalf of who cannot~ is incorrect because the preposition 'of' needs a noun or pronoun as its object before the relative clause. You must say [on behalf of those who] or [on behalf of people who].

cards.sideA: Si me hubieras escuchado 6 meses atrás, sería otra la historia.

sideB.text: If you had listened to me 6 months ago, it would be a different story.
If you'd listened to me six months ago, the story would be different.
If you had listened to me six months ago, things would be different.
If you had listened to me 6 months ago, it would be another story.

cards.note:
**[er:98]**

~If you were listened to me 6 months ago, it would be a different story~ is not valid because 'were listened' is a passive construction. To express an active past hypothetical condition, you must use the past perfect [had listened].

cards.sideA: Si hubiéramos hecho esto medio año atrás, sería bastante diferente.

sideB.text: If we had done this half a year ago, it would be quite different.
If we'd done this half a year ago, things would be quite different.
If we had done this six months ago, it would be a lot different.

cards.note:
**[er:98]**

**[er:151]**

This sentence uses a Mixed Conditional. It combines a past hypothetical condition (Past Perfect: had done) with a present imaginary result (would be).

~If we had done this a year and a half ago~ is grammatically correct but changes the time frame to 'un año y medio', whereas the card specifies 'medio año' [half a year].

~If we had done this one and a half year ago~ is incorrect because plural quantities require plural nouns like [years], and it also alters the original time frame.

cards.sideA: Estoy acostumbrado al frío.

sideB.text: I am used to the cold.
I'm accustomed to the cold.
I am accustomed to the cold weather.

cards.note:
**[er:15]**

~I am used to being the cold~ is incorrect because it literally translates to 'Estoy acostumbrado a ser el frío'. To express being accustomed to something, just follow the phrase directly with the noun: [I am used to the cold].

~I am used to being cold~ changes the meaning. It translates to 'Estoy acostumbrado a tener frío' (the physical sensation), whereas the card asks for 'Estoy acostumbrado al frío' (the environment), which is [I am used to the cold].

cards.sideA: Solía ir a fiestas cuando adolescente.

sideB.text: I used to go to parties as a teenager.
I used to go to parties when I was a teenager.
I would go to parties when I was a teen.
I used to go to parties when I was an adolescent.
I used to go to parties when I was young.

cards.sideA: Toda decisión tiene un costo.

sideB.text: Every decision has a trade-off.
Every choice comes with a trade-off.
Every decision involves a trade-off.
Every decision has a cost.

cards.note: A [trade-off] is a situation where you gain one thing but lose another. Some translations can be: intercambio, equilibrio, compensación, sacrificio, renuncia, costo.

cards.sideA: ¿Prometes pórtate bien?

sideB.text: Do you promise to behave?
Do you promise you'll behave?
Will you promise to behave well?

cards.sideA: Se portaron mal los niños.

sideB.text: The kids behaved badly.
The children misbehaved.
The kids behaved poorly.

cards.sideA: ¿Qué shell estás corriendo?

sideB.text: What shell are you running?
Which shell are you running?
What shell do you have running?

cards.sideA: ¿En qué shell vas a ejecutar esto — Bash, Zsh, PowerShell?

sideB.text: What shell are you going to run this in, Bash, Zsh, PowerShell?
Which shell will you run this in, Bash, Zsh, PowerShell?
In what shell are you going to run this, Bash, Zsh, PowerShell?

cards.sideA: ¿Mac o Windows? Así sé qué shell asumir.

sideB.text: Mac or Windows? That way I know which shell to assume.
Mac or Windows? That way I'll know what shell to assume.
Mac or Windows? So I know which shell to assume.

cards.sideA: ¿Qué tipo de shell tenés, así te doy los comandos correctos?

sideB.text: What type of shell do you have, so I can give you the correct commands?
What kind of shell do you have, so I can give you the right commands?
Which type of shell do you have, so I can give you the correct commands?

cards.sideA: Sh, Bash y Zsh son tipos de shell, igual que Gmail y Outlook son tipos de programa de correo.

sideB.text: Sh, Bash, and Zsh are types of shell, just like Gmail and Outlook are types of email program.
Sh, Bash, and Zsh are types of shells, just like Gmail and Outlook are types of email programs.
Sh, Bash, and Zsh are kinds of shell, the same way Gmail and Outlook are kinds of email program.

cards.sideA: "Shell" es la categoría; Bash es un ejemplo real dentro de esa categoría, no una pieza suya.

sideB.text: Shell is the category; Bash is an actual example within that category, not a piece of it.
Shell is the category, and Bash is a real example within it, not a part of it.
The word Shell is the category; Bash is an actual example within that category, not a piece of it.

cards.sideA: Si alguien te pregunta "¿qué es Bash?", la respuesta correcta es: "es un shell".

sideB.text: If someone asks what Bash is, the correct answer is it's a shell.
If someone asks what is Bash, the right answer is that it's a shell.
If someone asks you what Bash is, the correct answer is it's a shell.

cards.sideA: Bash y Zsh son el software real dentro de la categoría "Shell".

sideB.text: Bash and Zsh are the actual software within the Shell category.
Bash and Zsh are the real software within the Shell category.
Bash and Zsh are the actual software inside the Shell category.

cards.sideA: Es un script de comandos, no un programa compilado.

sideB.text: It's a script of commands, not a compiled program.
It's a command script, not a compiled program.
It is a script made of commands, not a compiled program.

cards.sideA: No es código como Java o Python; es una lista de instrucciones para el shell.

sideB.text: It's not code like Java or Python; it's a list of instructions for the shell.
It's not code like Java or Python, it's a list of instructions for the shell.
It isn't code like Java or Python; it's a set of instructions for the shell.

cards.sideA: Un script no se compila, se ejecuta línea por línea.

sideB.text: A script doesn't get compiled, it runs line by line.
A script isn't compiled; it's executed line by line.
A script doesn't compile, it runs line by line.

cards.sideA: El shell lee el script y va llamando a otros programas, como git o find.

sideB.text: The shell reads the script and calls other programs, like git or find.
The shell reads the script and goes calling other programs, like git or find.
The shell reads the script and invokes other programs, such as git or find.

cards.sideA: Una Terminal es solo una ventana, no entiende comandos por sí sola.

sideB.text: A Terminal is just a window, it doesn't understand commands by itself.
A Terminal is only a window; it doesn't understand commands on its own.
A Terminal is just a window, it can't understand commands by itself.

cards.sideA: El Shell es el programa que sí entiende y ejecuta los comandos.

sideB.text: The Shell is the program that actually understands and runs the commands.
The Shell is the program that really understands and executes the commands.
The Shell is the program that does understand and run the commands.

cards.sideA: El Shell corre adentro de la Terminal.

sideB.text: The Shell runs inside the Terminal.
The Shell runs inside of the Terminal.
The Shell runs within the Terminal.

cards.sideA: Bash, Zsh y sh son shells, no son "partes" de un shell.

sideB.text: Bash, Zsh, and sh are shells, not parts of a shell.
Bash, Zsh, and sh are shells, not pieces of a shell.
Bash, Zsh, and sh are shells themselves, not parts of a shell.

cards.sideA: Cada shell entiende comandos con su propia sintaxis.

sideB.text: Each shell understands commands using its own syntax.
Each shell understands commands with its own syntax.
Every shell understands commands using its own syntax.

cards.sideA: Mac y Linux usan shells tipo Unix; Windows usa PowerShell o CMD.

sideB.text: Mac and Linux use Unix-type shells; Windows uses PowerShell or CMD.
Mac and Linux use Unix-style shells; Windows uses PowerShell or CMD.
Mac and Linux use Unix-like shells, while Windows uses PowerShell or CMD.

cards.sideA: La extensión .sh no define qué shell ejecuta el archivo.

sideB.text: The .sh extension doesn't define which shell runs the file.
The .sh extension doesn't determine which shell runs the file.
The .sh extension does not define what shell runs the file.

cards.sideA: El shebang (primera línea del archivo) es lo que realmente decide el shell.

sideB.text: The shebang, the file's first line, is what really decides the shell.
The shebang, the first line of the file, is what actually decides the shell.
The shebang, the file's first line, is what really determines the shell.

cards.sideA: En Windows no existe shebang; ahí la extensión sí es la regla fija.

sideB.text: Windows has no shebang; there the extension itself is the fixed rule.
Windows doesn't have a shebang; there, the extension is the fixed rule.
On Windows there's no shebang; there the extension is indeed the fixed rule.

cards.sideA: WSL permite que Windows corra shells reales de Linux como Bash.

sideB.text: WSL lets Windows run real Linux shells like Bash.
WSL allows Windows to run real Linux shells like Bash.
WSL lets Windows run actual Linux shells such as Bash.

cards.sideA: Un shell es la interfaz entre el usuario y el sistema operativo.

sideB.text: A shell is the interface between the user and the operating system.
A shell acts as the interface between the user and the operating system.
A shell is an interface between the user and the operating system.

cards.sideA: sh significa shell.

sideB.text: sh means shell.
sh stands for shell.
sh is short for shell.

cards.sideA: Puedes escribir código bash dentro de un archivo shell.

sideB.text: You can write bash code inside a shell file.
You can write bash code inside of a shell file.
You can write Bash code within a shell file.

cards.sideA: Bash es un tipo específico de shell; por el contrario, un shell no es un tipo de Bash.

sideB.text: Bash is a specific type of shell; however, a shell is not a type of Bash.
Bash is a specific kind of shell; a shell, however, is not a kind of Bash.
Bash is one specific type of shell, but a shell is not a type of Bash.
